performance_overview: Through 8 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull has completed only 3 trades, all losers or state-repair: trade 1 = 2026-06-04 MPC long (-0.61% logged; true long ~-0.25%, stopped ~1 min after entry on a too-tight ultra-low-ATR stop), trade 2 = 2026-06-05 corrective buy-to-cover of the unintended -18 MPC short from the 06-04 double-sell (+0.54%), trade 3 = today's 2026-06-10 CIEN long (-1.68%, stopped out ~21 min after entry). So the clean signal-driven sample is now ~1-2 trades and 0 wins; the 'win rate' buckets are still noise. Today (06-10) Bull finished -0.08% (-$83.30, the entire CIEN stop loss) while SPY closed -1.37%, so +1.29% relative on the day. Since inception cumulative Bull -0.0766% vs SPY -4.17%: Bull remains well ahead of SPY cumulatively, but entirely from staying near-flat/cautious through a risk-off stretch, not from realized trading gains. Trend over last 5 sessions: four flat no-trade days then today's single small losing trade; selectivity still the only edge.
overnight_hold_insights: Still ZERO genuine overnight holds completed across all 8 sessions. The overnight_holds.closed_eod bucket now reads 3 trades / 1W / 33.3% / avg -0.58%, but it is contaminated: all three entries are intraday/EOD closes (06-04 MPC long close, 06-05 corrective short-cover, today's CIEN stop-out), none a real carry-overnight outcome. No gap-risk data exists yet. Carry-decision policy unchanged: apply the 6 config safe-hold gates (profitable, trailing stop active, price > EMA-21, no earnings within 2 days, sentiment not negative, gap-risk cushion). Standing lesson: low-ATR or red/gap-down names that don't extend within the session are correctly closed/stopped rather than carried.

No overnight holds, nothing to manage at the open. Account healthy: equity $99,923.37, cash $99,923.37, daytrade_count 0, NOT PDT, no kill switch, no flags. (b) WATCHLIST + RESEARCH WERE CLEARED at EOD (Part 8) -- the 8:30 AM scan rebuilds from scratch; do not re-evaluate any prior names. (c) TODAY'S KEY LESSON (CIEN): CIEN passed every mechanical gate at the 10:15 entry (RSI 62.1, rel_vol 1.25, MACD rising, EMA9>EMA21, above EMA-200, atr% 0.40%, positive sentiment) yet still lost -1.68% in ~21 min because it was a FAILED BOUNCE on a name that was RED ~-4% vs prev_close and gapped down -- it bounced into the entry, rolled over, and lost the 1.5xATR stop ($444.51 -> filled $443.20). This is the SAME shape that midday correctly declined on TER. Reinforced rule: do NOT chase intraday bounces on names that are red/gapped-down on the day even when EMA9>EMA21 + MACD-rising + rel_vol gates pass; demand the name be green / above prev_close / clearly above VWAP at entry, not just mechanically confirmed. Avoid ultra-low-ATR (<0.10%) hair-trigger names (MPC profile) too. (d) CODE BUG STILL OPEN (since 06-04): CIEN's stop FILLED at 14:31:13Z @ $443.20 but the EXIT was NEVER logged to trade_log.jsonl at midday -- positions.json was reconciled to {} but the exit record was dropped, so update_memory.py would have missed the trade entirely. I reconstructed and appended the EXIT from broker order history (tc.get_orders) so the stats bucketed correctly (now total_trades 3). The close/EOD/midday logic must (i) reconcile positions.json against tc.get_all_positions() AND (ii) WRITE the EXIT to trade_log.jsonl whenever a protective stop fills, BEFORE any further action. Until fixed, every agent should cross-check broker order history against trade_log for unlogged stop fills. VIX elevated ~22, SPY sold off into the close -- regime softening; if SPY EMA9 crosses below EMA21 tomorrow consider the regime-down position cap. (g) Selectivity remains the only edge (Bull -0.08% cumulative vs SPY -4.17%, all from staying in cash). Do not force entries; the first WIN should be a high-conviction, ATR-healthy, GREEN/confirmed name -- not a red-tape bounce.
